Certain corporations making great parade of the
fact that few of hem because of the general prosperity
and increased cost of living have voluntarily raised the
wages of employees.


No opportunity is lost for laying emphasis on
fhe fact that American labor is better paid than is the
labor of most other countries.


Such assertion is sort Of spread gleism that is
supposed to hurt nobody and to help the corporations
a more complacent enjoyment of their profits.


But the real truth is that measured by the value of
Its product the labor Of this country, instead of being
the highest paid, is the lowest paid in the world


Census reports show that in American manufactures
The per capita production is three times the average of
European factories while wages less than twice
high


James J. Hill, president of the Great Northern and
other railroads. is authority for the assertion that train
gang his American railroads handles more than seven
times many miles of freight do the train gangs
seven times much result American trainmen receive
Jess than twice much wages.
Secretary Wilson, of the department of agriculture
In address the other day. said that American farm
band produces more rice than 400 Cinnamon Chinese
wages only 10 to II cents day, but if the rice
workers of this country got the rate for results
produced their pay would be $44 to $4S dayinstead
pf $3.50.


"Cleap labor'' never could produce the marvellous
drops and manufactures of this country Yet measured
by what it does it is the cheapest labor the surface
pf Che earth.


And is utterly false that prices are high because
0ages are high.


Compared with the prices the pays, wages
pre- lower thar. ip Europe or China.
